input_dict.question: Are both Anglo-Français de Petite Vénerie and Hawaiian Poi Dog extinct breeds of dogs?
input_dict.source_summary: No — the Hawaiian Poi Dog is extinct, while the Anglo‑Français de Petite Vénerie is not described as extinct in the provided evidence. Anglo‑Français de Petite Vénerie is described as a medium-sized Anglo‑French scenthound used to hunt small game (extinction/status not given in the provided passages). Hawaiian Poi Dog is an extinct pariah dog from Hawaiʻi that was kept by Native Hawaiians as a spiritual protector of children and as a food source.
input_dict.target_summary:
Integrated evidence summary:

- Anglo‑Français de Petite Vénerie — The Anglo‑Français de Petite Vénerie is a medium-sized breed of dog used in hunting as a scenthound, usually in packs. It is one of the Anglo‑French hound breeds created by crossing French scenthounds with English (Anglo) foxhounds. The name "Petite Vénerie" refers to its use in hunting small game. (No extinction or current conservation/status is stated in the provided Anglo‑Français passages.)

- Hawaiian Poi Dog — The Hawaiian Poi Dog is described in the earlier summary as an extinct breed of pariah dog from Hawaiʻi that was kept by Native Hawaiians as a spiritual protector of children and as a source of food.

Note: The Anglo‑Français evidence supplied characterizes breed, origin, and purpose but does not specify whether the breed is extinct or extant; the Hawaiian Poi Dog is explicitly described as extinct in the provided material.
input_dict.candidate_summary: No — the Hawaiian Poi Dog is extinct, while the Anglo‑Français de Petite Vénerie is not described as extinct in the provided evidence. Anglo‑Français de Petite Vénerie is described as a medium-sized scenthound used to hunt small game (extinction/status not given in the provided passages). Hawaiian Poi Dog is described as an extinct pariah dog from Hawaiʻi.
